title: UK Government Launches Â£2.5B DRIVE35 Programme to Power UK Auto Investment (ZEV) and Jobs
content: The UK government has launched DRIVE35, a Â£2.5 billion programme to boost the domestic auto industryâ€™s transition to zero-emission vehicle (ZEV) manufacturing. As part of the UKâ€™s modern Industrial Strategy, the package includes Â£2 billion in funding through 2030 and an additional Â£500 million for R&D through 2035. The initiative aims to support projects across the supply chainâ€”from gigafactories to startupsâ€”providing long-term certainty for investors and innovators. Alongside this, more than Â£300 million has been allocated to specific auto projects, including over Â£100 million to Astemo Ltd. in Bolton for EV component production, and Â£15 million to Dana in the West Midlands. The UK was Europeâ€™s largest EV market in 2024, with over 382,000 EVs sold. DRIVE35 builds on the success of the Automotive Transformation Fund and the Advanced Propulsion Centre, which previously leveraged over Â£6 billion in private investment.
author: Analysts Account
